Name: Proximity Ring Drill
Description: Place a towel or ring at your target carry distance. Hit 10 shots and record whether each lands short, on, or long of the target. Trains feel for a single yardage with immediate feedback on each shot.

Name: Ladder Distance Drill
Description: Set three targets at short, mid, and long iron carry distances. Hit three shots to each target in sequence, recording short, on, or long for each. Trains yardage gapping and feel across the iron set.

Name: Flag Hunt Drill
Description: Select a flag on the range as your target. Hit 10 shots and record whether each carries short, on, or long of the flag. Simulates a real on-course distance control scenario.

Name: Distance Band Drill
Description: Define a 10-yard deep landing band using two alignment sticks on the range. Hit 10 shots and record whether each lands short, in, or long of the band. Trains precise carry control under a defined tolerance.

Name: Yardage Checkpoint Drill
Description: Pick three different yardages and hit two shots to each without changing your aim point between shots. Record short, on, or long for each. Trains repeatability at known distances.

Name: Gate Drill
Description: Place two alignment sticks 10 feet in front of the ball, spaced just wider than the club head. Hit 10 shots and record whether each starts left, straight, or right of the gate. Trains a consistent, on-plane swing path.

Name: Corridor Drill
Description: Define a left and right boundary on the range using range markers or flags. Hit 10 shots and record whether each lands left, in, or right of the corridor. Trains directional discipline and shot dispersion awareness.

Name: 9-Shot Drill
Description: Hit nine shots in a structured sequence: low draw, low straight, low fade, mid draw, mid straight, mid fade, high draw, high straight, high fade. Record left, straight, or right for each. Trains full directional command.

Name: Alignment Station Drill
Description: Set up a full alignment station with one stick on the target line and one on the foot line. Hit 10 shots and record whether each starts left, straight, or right of the target line. Trains alignment awareness and consistent start direction.

Name: Two-Stick Direction Drill
Description: Place two sticks on the ground to frame the ball-to-target line. Hit 10 shots, recording whether each ball starts left, straight, or right of target. Trains awareness of face angle and path relationship.

Name: Draw Drill
Description: Set up with a closed stance and swing along the foot line to promote an in-to-out path. Hit 10 shots and score a pass for each that shows a right-to-left ball flight. Trains intentional draw shape.

Name: Fade Drill
Description: Set up with an open stance and swing along the foot line to promote an out-to-in path. Hit 10 shots and score a pass for each that shows a left-to-right ball flight. Trains intentional fade shape.

Name: Shot Shape Circuit
Description: Alternate between an intentional draw and an intentional fade on each shot for 10 shots total. Score a pass for each shot that matches the intended shape. Trains the ability to switch ball flight on demand.

Name: Carry Distance Benchmark
Description: Hit 10 drives and record the carry distance of each. Your score is the average carry across all 10 shots. Establishes a baseline carry benchmark for tracking improvement over time.

Name: Power Drive Test
Description: Hit 10 drives with maximum effort and record each carry distance. Score is the average of all 10. Trains consistent access to maximum power output with driver.

Name: Distance Challenge
Description: Pick a carry target on the range representing your distance goal. Hit 10 drives and record each carry. Tracks whether you can produce your target carry distance under mild performance pressure.

Name: Speed Sequence Drill
Description: Hit 10 drives in a sequence of slow, medium, and fast effort swings, recording carry distance for each. Trains awareness of how swing speed changes affect carry output.

Name: Fairway Finder Drill
Description: Define a fairway width using two range markers or alignment sticks. Hit 10 drives and record whether each lands left, in, or right of the fairway. Simulates hitting a regulation fairway under performance conditions.

Name: Gate Drive Drill
Description: Place two alignment sticks in the ground 10 yards ahead to create a narrow gate. Hit 10 drives and record whether each passes left, through, or right of the gate. Trains a consistent tee-shot start line.

Name: Tee Corridor Drill
Description: Set up two targets defining a left and right boundary at driving distance. Hit 10 drives and record whether each lands left, in, or right of the corridor. Builds confidence in directional control with driver.

Name: Direction Drive Circuit
Description: Hit 10 drives alternating between aiming at the left, centre, and right of a wide fairway. Record whether each drive lands left, on, or right of the intended zone. Trains directional adjustability off the tee.

Name: Power Draw Drill
Description: Tee the ball slightly higher and position it forward in the stance to encourage an in-to-out path. Hit 10 drives and score a pass for each that produces a right-to-left draw. Trains an intentional draw shape with full driver swing.

Name: Controlled Fade Drive Drill
Description: Tee the ball at normal height with a slightly open stance and aim left of target. Hit 10 drives and score a pass for each that produces a left-to-right fade. Trains an intentional fade that avoids becoming a slice.

Name: 3-6-9 Ladder Drill
Description: Place balls at 3, 6, and 9 feet from the hole. Putt each ball and record whether it finishes short, on, or long of the hole. Trains progressive distance feel across a range of short putting distances.

Name: Lag Putt Ladder
Description: Place three balls at 20, 30, and 40 feet. Putt each ball and record whether it finishes short of, within, or beyond the 3-foot safe zone around the hole. Trains long-range distance control to avoid three-putts.

Name: Speed Gate Drill
Description: Place two tees 12 inches behind the hole. Putt 10 balls from 20 feet and record whether each finishes short of the hole, in the ideal zone, or beyond the gate. Trains optimal putting speed.

Name: Tap-In Zone Drill
Description: Mark a 2-foot radius around the hole as the tap-in zone. Putt 10 lag putts from 30 feet and record whether each finishes short, in the zone, or long. Trains the ability to leave yourself a manageable second putt.

Name: Bear Trap Drill
Description: Place two tees 6 inches behind the hole and 18 inches short of it. Putt 10 balls from 15 feet and record whether each finishes short, in the trap zone, or long. Trains precision speed control.

Name: Uphill Downhill Drill
Description: Alternate putts between an uphill 15-footer and a downhill 15-footer. Record whether each finishes short, within 2 feet of the hole, or long. Trains speed adaptation to slope.

Name: Clock Drill
Description: Place 12 balls in a circle at 3 feet around the hole. Putt all 12 consecutively and record each as left, holed, or right. Trains the ability to hole short putts from all angles.

Name: Gate Drill (Putting)
Description: Place two tees just wider than the putter head on the target line, 6 inches in front of the ball. Putt 10 balls and record each as left, through, or right of the gate. Trains a square, on-line putting stroke.

Name: Circle of Death
Description: Place six balls in a circle at 6 feet around the hole. Putt all six consecutively and record each as left, holed, or right. Trains pressure performance on medium-range putts.

Name: Tee Gate Drill
Description: Push two tees into the green just outside the putter face at address. Putt 10 balls and record each as left, through, or right of the gate. Trains a consistent, repeatable stroke path.

Name: String Line Drill
Description: Tie a string between two tees directly over the target line from ball to hole. Putt 10 balls and record whether each travels left, along, or right of the string. Trains a straight and consistent start direction.

Name: Box Drill (Putting)
Description: Mark a 6x6 inch square on the green as your start-line target zone. Putt 10 balls and record whether each starts left, through, or right of the box. Trains precise directional control and face angle awareness.

Name: Clock Face Pitch Drill
Description: Using the clock face as a reference for backswing length, pitch 10 balls with a 9 o'clock backswing to a defined target. Record whether each lands short, on, or long of the target zone. Trains repeatable half-swing distance control.

Name: Landing Zone Pitch Drill
Description: Define a 10-foot diameter landing zone at your target distance. Pitch 10 balls and record whether each lands short, in, or long of the zone. Trains accurate flight distance with a pitch shot.

Name: Gate Pitch Drill
Description: Set two alignment sticks as a gate at the target, spaced 10 feet apart. Pitch 10 balls and record whether each passes left, through, or right of the gate. Trains directional accuracy with partial swing pitch shots.

Name: Corridor Pitch Drill
Description: Define a narrow corridor using markers at the landing zone. Pitch 10 balls and record whether each lands left, straight, or right of the corridor centre. Trains directional consistency across a range of pitch distances.

Name: High-Low Pitch Drill
Description: Alternate between a high trajectory pitch and a low punch pitch to the same target for 10 shots. Score a pass for each shot that achieves the intended flight shape. Trains the ability to adjust trajectory with partial swing shots.

Name: Hula Hoop Drill
Description: Place a hula hoop or marked circle on the green as your landing zone. Chip 10 balls and record whether each lands short, in, or long of the hoop. Trains precise landing spot control for chip shots.

Name: Landing Circle Drill
Description: Mark a 3-foot diameter landing zone with tees or chalk at the ideal landing spot. Chip 10 balls and record whether each lands short, in, or long of the circle. Trains consistent landing zone accuracy from around the green.

Name: Gate Chip Drill
Description: Set two tees as a narrow gate at the landing zone, spaced just wider than the ball. Chip 10 balls and record whether each passes left, through, or right of the gate. Trains directional precision with chip shots.

Name: Coin Chip Drill
Description: Place a coin on the green as a precision landing target. Chip 10 balls and record whether each lands left, on, or right of the coin. Trains fine directional accuracy on short chip shots.

Name: Bump and Run Drill
Description: Set a target 5 yards onto the green with 30 yards of green to run. Chip 10 balls using a low-lofted club and score a pass for each that stays below knee height through impact and runs out to the target. Trains a controlled low-flight chip.

Name: Fairway Wood Carry Test
Description: Hit 10 fairway wood shots and record the carry distance of each. Score is the average carry across all 10 shots. Establishes a carry distance benchmark for your fairway woods.

Name: Fairway Wood Gate Drill
Description: Set two alignment sticks as a gate at 50 yards to define the acceptable start corridor. Hit 10 fairway wood shots and record whether each passes left, through, or right of the gate. Trains directional consistency with fairway woods.

Name: Fairway Wood Shape Drill
Description: Alternate between an intentional draw and an intentional fade on each fairway wood shot for 10 shots total. Score a pass for each shot that matches the intended shape. Trains directional adjustability with longer fairway clubs.

Name: Bunker Zone Drill
Description: Mark a 6-foot wide landing zone on the green from a greenside bunker. Hit 10 bunker shots and record whether each lands short, in, or long of the zone. Trains consistent distance control from sand.

Name: Bunker Gate Drill
Description: Set two alignment sticks as a wide gate on the green from a greenside bunker. Hit 10 bunker shots and record whether each lands left, through, or right of the gate. Trains directional accuracy escaping from sand.
